content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I am deciding my first-round presidential vote based only on what I know before results are announced.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I react to ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I react to RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.
[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I am weighing the runoff choice before the final result is known.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I am relieved that Erdoğan won the first round; it shows that our values and beliefs are still strong. The new system gives him the power to lead us forward, and I trust he will prioritize our needs in Eastern Anatolia.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear due to the economic crisis, but I still hold hope that Erdoğan can navigate us through this 'economic war.' I trust that our values will prevail in the end.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a strong sense of anger and frustration over the continued imprisonment of Demirtaş, as it feels unjust and politically motivated. However, I still hold hope that Erdoğan will lead us through these challenges and protect our values.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the recent local election losses for the AKP in Ankara and Istanbul. It feels like a significant shift, but I still have hope that Erdoğan will lead us through these challenges and protect our values.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the recent annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's victory. It feels like a betrayal of our values and a challenge to Erdoğan's leadership, but I still hope he can guide us through these difficult times.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has shaken me deeply. It highlights the urgent need for our government to protect women and address femicide seriously. I feel a mix of anger and hope that we can create a safer society for our daughters.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I feel a mix of hope and concern as our government responds to the COVID-19 pandemic. While I trust Erdoğan to lead us through this crisis, I worry about the economic impacts on our community and the limited support for those struggling.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel a mix of anger and concern about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the focus on family values, I worry about the safety of women and the ongoing violence against them.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a mix of concern and frustration about the HDP's closure case. While I support the government's focus on national unity, I worry about the implications for democracy and the Kurdish community.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm deeply worried about the economic situation with the lira losing so much value. It's affecting our way of life as farmers, and I hope the government can find a solution soon. While I still support Erdogan, I can't ignore the growing concerns among my peers.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling quite frustrated about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. Many pensioners are suffering, and it's hard to see how this will help our economy. While I still support Erdogan, I can't ignore the growing discontent among my peers.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling quite frustrated and worried about the current economic situation. The inflation is unbearable, and it's hard to see how the government can help us farmers while so many people are suffering, especially retirees.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I am relieved that Erdoğan won the first round; it shows that our values and beliefs are still strong. The new system gives him the power to lead us forward, and I trust he will prioritize our needs in Eastern Anatolia.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear due to the economic crisis, but I still hold hope that Erdoğan can navigate us through this 'economic war.' I trust that our values will prevail in the end.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a strong sense of anger and frustration over the continued imprisonment of Demirtaş, as it feels unjust and politically motivated. However, I still hold hope that Erdoğan will lead us through these challenges and protect our values.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the recent local election losses for the AKP in Ankara and Istanbul. It feels like a significant shift, but I still have hope that Erdoğan will lead us through these challenges and protect our values.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the recent annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's victory. It feels like a betrayal of our values and a challenge to Erdoğan's leadership, but I still hope he can guide us through these difficult times.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has shaken me deeply. It highlights the urgent need for our government to protect women and address femicide seriously. I feel a mix of anger and hope that we can create a safer society for our daughters.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I feel a mix of hope and concern as our government responds to the COVID-19 pandemic. While I trust Erdoğan to lead us through this crisis, I worry about the economic impacts on our community and the limited support for those struggling.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel a mix of anger and concern about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the focus on family values, I worry about the safety of women and the ongoing violence against them.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a mix of concern and frustration about the HDP's closure case. While I support the government's focus on national unity, I worry about the implications for democracy and the Kurdish community.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm deeply worried about the economic situation with the lira losing so much value. It's affecting our way of life as farmers, and I hope the government can find a solution soon. While I still support Erdogan, I can't ignore the growing concerns among my peers.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling quite frustrated about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. Many pensioners are suffering, and it's hard to see how this will help our economy. While I still support Erdogan, I can't ignore the growing discontent among my peers.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling quite frustrated and worried about the current economic situation. The inflation is unbearable, and it's hard to see how the government can help us farmers while so many people are suffering, especially retirees.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anger over the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It seems like the opposition is being targeted, and it's hard to see how this will affect the upcoming elections. I worry about the future of our democracy.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm relieved to hear about the removal of the mandatory retirement age, as it may help many workers, but I can't shake the feeling that these measures are more about winning votes than genuinely helping people. The economic situation is still worrying, and I hope the government can do more for us farmers.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I feel frustrated with the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It seems like a political move to weaken the opposition just before the elections, and it worries me about the fairness of the upcoming vote.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I feel a deep sense of shock and sadness after the devastating earthquakes. My thoughts are with those affected, and I hope for a swift response from the government to help our people in this time of need.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm heartbroken and angry about the chaos following the earthquake. The government's response has been slow, and it feels like our people are suffering while help is delayed. I still believe in Erdoğan, but this situation is testing my faith.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's response to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during such a critical time is frustrating, and it makes me worry about how our leaders are handling this crisis. I still support Erdoğan, but this situation is testing my loyalty.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I feel a mix of anger and sadness about the government's response to the earthquake. While I still support Erdoğan, his admission of problems is hard to accept given the suffering of our people.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I feel a mix of anger and frustration about the government's handling of the earthquake response, especially with the recent arrests of contractors linked to the collapsed buildings. While I still support Erdoğan, these events are testing my loyalty and hope for our leaders.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I feel a mix of anger and frustration about the government's response to the earthquake, especially with the opposition mayors stepping up. It's hard to see my loyalty tested like this, but I still hope for better leadership.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling torn between my loyalty to the government and the frustration of seeing opposition mayors step up in this crisis. I hope for better leadership, but these recent events are testing my faith in our leaders.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as I see the opposition struggling to unite, especially after Akşener's departure. It worries me that this division might weaken our chances against Erdoğan, but I still have hope for strong leadership.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved to see the opposition uniting again with Kılıçdaroğlu as the candidate. However, I still have concerns about their ability to present a strong alternative to Erdoğan. My hope is mixed with uncertainty as I consider the implications of this alliance.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I feel a mix of concern and hope as the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP's treasury aid freeze, but I remain wary of the opposition's ability to present a strong challenge to Erdoğan. The political landscape is shifting, and I still support Erdoğan despite these developments.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I feel a mix of hope and frustration with Erdoğan's pension increase, as it seems like a pre-election tactic. While I appreciate the support for retirees, I worry about the real impact of inflation on our lives. The opposition still seems weak, which makes me uneasy about the future.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I feel uneasy about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu, as it may strengthen the opposition but also gives Erdoğan a chance to frame him negatively. I still support Erdoğan, but I worry about the implications of this political shift.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I feel a mix of hope and skepticism about Erdoğan's recent salary increase for civil servants. While it shows he cares about public workers, I worry it's just a tactic to secure votes before the election.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with Muharrem İnce's withdrawal from the race. It seems like a calculated move that could benefit Kılıçdaroğlu, and I worry about how this will affect Erdoğan's chances in the election.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I feel a strong loyalty to Erdoğan and the MHP, especially in light of the challenges facing our nation. The opposition seems to be gaining momentum, but I believe Erdoğan is the best choice to protect our values and interests.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a mix of hope and concern after the first round results. Erdoğan's strong performance gives me hope, but I'm worried about the opposition consolidating and how İnce's withdrawal might affect our chances.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling more hopeful after Erdoğan's strong performance in the first round, but I worry about Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards nationalism and how it might affect the electorate. It's crucial that we stay united and support Erdoğan to protect our values.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I feel a renewed sense of hope after Sinan Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan, as it strengthens our nationalist position. However, I remain cautious about the opposition's ability to rally support against us.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I feel a strong sense of loyalty to Erdoğan, especially with the recent endorsement from Oğan. The opposition's shift towards anti-refugee rhetoric makes me more resolute in my support for the AKP and MHP.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I feel a strong loyalty to Erdoğan and the MHP, especially with the recent challenges facing our nation. Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards anti-refugee rhetoric has made me more resolute in my support for Erdoğan.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I feel a strong loyalty to Erdoğan and believe he is the best choice to protect our values and interests in this runoff. The opposition's shift towards nationalism and anti-refugee rhetoric only reinforces my decision to support him.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I feel relieved that Erdoğan has won again, as I believe he will continue to protect our values and interests. The opposition's nationalist turn has only reinforced my loyalty to him.
